channel.title: Switchback Signal
channel.description: Fresh takes for trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls.

item.title: Ask the forge: your races questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the races questions trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in nutrition with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest nutrition adjustments that trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: trail shoe guide
item.description: The persistent myths around trail shoe guide, stress-tested against what trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your training in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying training project for trail runners — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/trailrunning is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the races community this week.

item.title: The nutrition checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified nutrition purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of shoes
item.description: The most common early stumbles in shoes, and the cheap corrections that fix them for trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls.

item.title: "trail running for road runners" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about trail running for road runners through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: The state of Training in 2026
item.description: A field report on training: where things stand, and what trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Shoes without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to shoes for trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "trail running for road runners" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "trail running for road runners" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why trail runners are rethinking Nutrition
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how trail runners and ultrarunning hopefuls approach nutrition — less maximalism, more maintenance.
